---
title: Swing Decision
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# include for setup 
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(ggplot2)
library(rmarkdown)
library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(DT)
library(data.table)

game <- read_csv("20260213-GeorgiaState-1_unverified.csv") # Copy and paste whichever csv you're looking at here
hitter <- "Bass, Wesley" # Put the hitter here in the FORM RECOGNIZED BY TRACKMAN.
titleHitter <- str_replace_all(hitter, "(\\w+), (\\w+)", "\\2 \\1") # creates the title from the Hitter, switching the position of the first and last name.
titleDate <- game$UTCDate[1]  #pulls the date from the csv
titleOpponent <- game$AwayTeam[1] # pulls opponent from the csv (if an opponent shared their csv from a road game, change this to game$HomeTeam[1])
gsuBatter <- subset(game, Batter==hitter) # filters data using selected Hitter

# Lines 26-30 put the Hitter, Logo, Opponent, and Date at the top of the page
# Lines 33-40 create a 2 column layout that takes up 100% of the screen for side-by-side content to be split 50-50 (the two </div> at the bottom of the code "close" each row and column that were set up)
```

  <div style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center;"> 
  <div style="flex: 1; text-align: left; font-weight: bold; font-size: 20px;">`r   titleHitter`</div> 
  <div style="flex: 1; text-align: center;"><img src="panther.png" style="max-width: 75px; max-height: 75px;"></div>  
  <div style="flex: 1; text-align: right; font-weight: bold; font-size: 20px;">`r titleOpponent` - `r titleDate`</div> 
  </div> 
  
   <style type="text/css"> 
    .main-container {max-width: 100%;} 
    .row {display: flex;} 
    .column {flex: 50%;} 
 </style> 

 <div class = "row"> 
 <div class = "column">
 
```{r table, fig.align = 'left', echo=FALSE}
# create table by selecting and renaming variables
tableData <- gsuBatter %>%
  select(Inning, Pitcher, PitchofPA, Balls, Strikes, RelSpeed, TaggedPitchType, PitchCall,
          ExitSpeed) %>%
   rename("Pitch #" = PitchofPA, Inn = Inning, Call = PitchCall, EV = ExitSpeed, 
          Velo = RelSpeed) %>%
   unite(Count, c("Balls", "Strikes"), sep = "-") %>%
   mutate(across(c('EV'), round, 1), across(c('Velo'), round, 1), 
          Call = replace(Call, Call == "BallCalled", "BC"), 
          Call = replace(Call, Call == "StrikeCalled", "KC"),
          Call = replace(Call, Call == "FoulBallNotFieldable", "FB"),
          Call = replace(Call, Call == "FoulBallFieldable", "FB"),
          Call = replace(Call, Call == "StrikeSwinging", "KS"),
          Call = replace(Call, Call == "HitByPitch", "HBP"),
          Call = replace(Call, Call =="InPlay", "IP"),
          EV = replace(EV, EV == -1000, " "),
          TaggedPitchType = replace(TaggedPitchType, TaggedPitchType == "Fastball", "FB"),
          TaggedPitchType = replace(TaggedPitchType, TaggedPitchType == "Sinker", "FB"),
          TaggedPitchType = replace(TaggedPitchType, TaggedPitchType == "Curveball", "CB"),
          TaggedPitchType = replace(TaggedPitchType, TaggedPitchType == "Cutter", "CUT"),
          TaggedPitchType = replace(TaggedPitchType, TaggedPitchType == "CurveBall", "CB"),
          TaggedPitchType = replace(TaggedPitchType, TaggedPitchType == "Slider", "SL"),
          TaggedPitchType = replace(TaggedPitchType, TaggedPitchType == "ChangeUp", "CH"),
          TaggedPitchType = replace(TaggedPitchType, TaggedPitchType == "Splitter", "SPL")) %>%
  unite(Call, c("TaggedPitchType", "Call"), sep=" - ") 

# Create a column to indicate row separators using shift
tableData <- tableData %>%
  mutate(Row_Separator = ifelse(is.na(lag(Inn)) | Inn != lag(Inn), "yes", "no"))

# datatable() allows table to be displayed as HTML, so the following code is in R and Javascript
datatable(
  tableData,
  rownames = FALSE, # deletes row numbers on left hand side
  options = list(
    dom = 't', # shows only table (hides search bar, page length menu, info text...)
    paging = FALSE, # makes sure table is on one page
    columnDefs = list(
      list(
        className = 'dt-center', 
        targets = "_all" # these two lines center the columns
      ),
      list(
        visible = FALSE,
        targets = ncol(tableData) - 1 # these two lines hide the last column of the table (the row separator column)
      )
    ),
    initComplete = JS(
      'function(settings, json) {
      var api = this.api();
      var rows = api.rows({page: "current"}).nodes();
      var last = null;
      
      api.column(0, {page: "current"}).data().each(function(group,i){
        if (group != last){
          $(rows).eq(i).before(
            "<tr class=\\"group\\\"><td colspan=\\"8\\" style=\\"border-top: 2px solid black; text-align: center;\\"></td></tr>"
          );
          last = group;
        }
      });
      }' # end of javascript code, purpose was to put horizontal lines in table (as soon as you filter the table, the lines disappear and won't come back unless you re-knit)
    )
  ),
  width = "85%" # this number is changeable
)

# lines 113-121 change the font for the table. line 123 opens the second column for the following plot
```

 <style> 
   /* Adjust font size for all cells and header row */ 
  .datatables table td,
  .datatables table th {
    font-size: 8px !important;
  }
</style>

</div>

<div class = "column">
```{r Charts, echo=FALSE, fig.align='right' ,fig.width=10, fig.height=10}
# this code creates the plot for the ABs

gsuBatter$TaggedPitchType <- factor(gsuBatter$TaggedPitchType, levels = c("Fastball", "Sinker", "Cutter", "Slider", "Curveball", "ChangeUp", "Splitter")) # The options for the legend.

gsuBatter$PitchCall <- factor(gsuBatter$PitchCall, levels = c("BallCalled", "StrikeCalled", "StrikeSwinging", "FoulBallNotFieldable", "FoulBallFieldable", "InPlay", "HitByPitch")) # The options for the legend.

ggplot(gsuBatter) +
  aes(PlateLocSide, PlateLocHeight, col=PitchCall, shape =TaggedPitchType, 
      label=PitchofPA) +
  geom_point(size=7) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Fastball" = 16, "Sinker" = 16, "Slider" = 17, 
                                "Curveball" = 17, "Cutter" = 18, "ChangeUp" = 15, 
                                "Splitter" = 15)) +
  scale_color_manual(values= c("BallCalled" = "green", "StrikeCalled"="purple",
                               "FoulBallNotFieldable"="orange", 
                               "FoulBallFieldable"="orange", "InPlay"="yellow",
                               "StrikeSwinging"="red", "HitByPitch" = "pink")) +
  geom_text(hjust=0.5, vjust=0.5, color="black") +
  ggtitle("Plate Appearances by Inning") +
  geom_rect(mapping = aes(ymax = 3.37750, ymin = 1.5, xmax = -.83083, xmin = .83083)
            , alpha = 0, size=1.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_rect(mapping = aes(ymax = 3.5, ymin = 1.37750, xmax = -.99750, xmin = .99750), 
            alpha = 0, size=1.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_rect(mapping = aes(ymax = 5, ymin = 0, xmax = -1.8, xmin = 1.8), 
            alpha = 0, size=0.5, colour = "black") +
  geom_rect(mapping = aes(ymax = 3, ymin = 2, xmax = -.41666667, xmin = .41666667), 
            alpha=0, size=1.2, colour = "red")+
  facet_wrap(~Inning) + ylab("") + xlab("") +
  theme_bw()
```

 </div>
 </div>
